parsed_review.summary: CP00-487 is a descriptor-only governance-core P02 test-slice pack (RP16.P02.M07.S03-S12, risk A, 10 units, deliverables implementation:6/security_audit:2/ui:2) and is sound. Pack identity, scope, distributions, and the "Test And Golden Case Set" section (10 descriptor-only rows) are consistent across manifest, baseline, plan, registry binding, contract projection, and the validator, which deepEquals contract against code output. The pack does not over-claim: manifest production_ready=false/runtime_ready=false with a scoped descriptor flag; the Hermes production_ready_candidate is gated solely on structural coverage/descriptor validity. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced through a frozen attestation chain (base CP480) and freezeCp487Result flags, with in-scope source being pure frozen-object construction (no fs/db/network) and tests reading only. Claude remains a read-only reviewer (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, no enterprise-trust-from-local-validation, tools Read/Grep/Glob) and is not final approver; Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic descriptor review-packet/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false). CP178+ review hardening (auth-fail, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped, missing-field rejection) is encoded in forbidden_review_evidence and the auth preflight passed with valid JSON. Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook docs) are correctly excluded from pack scope; no required untracked implementation files were omitted. One non-blocking informational note: downstream hardening artifacts (adjudication, construction-inspection, claude-review-result) and post-review closeout-pack:validate are not yet present at review time, which is expected as they are downstream of this review step.

findings.title: Post-review hardening artifacts and gated validation not present at review time (expected, non-blocking)
findings.details: The manifest lists evidence_refs/required_pack_artifacts including claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, and the baseline validation_commands include npm run closeout-pack:validate. At review time only manifest.json and command-evidence.json exist under docs/closeout-packs/cp00-487/, and command-evidence.json (notes) explicitly classifies closeout-pack:review:run/normalize/receipt:validate, closeout-pack:validate, and git diff --check --cached as post-review gated commands. This is consistent with the hardening_sequence, where finding_adjudication, construction_inspection, and final_closeout_validation run after this read-only review step. It is therefore an expected state, not a defect: I can directly confirm support for the production_ready=false descriptor claim from implementation, tests, Hermes/local validation, and no-write/no-real-data boundaries, but adjudication and construction-inspection evidence cannot be verified by this review because they are produced downstream of it.
findings.recommendation: Complete the remaining hardening steps (finding adjudication, construction inspection, final closeout validation including npm run closeout-pack:validate, and git diff --check --cached) and generate the listed post-review artifacts before committing the pack. No change to the implementation is required.
